articles.title: Fixation duration on natural scenes is explained by memory encoding not processing demand
articles.snippet: By combining magnetoencephalography and eye tracking, this study sheds light on why people fixate on some parts of natural scenes longer than others. Rather than visual complexity, fixation durations are affected by memory encoding.
